title: stenandrium arnoldii
intro: Stenandrium arnoldii, also known as the "wild basil" or "forest basil", is a plant species native to tropical regions. It is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Acanthaceae family and has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Stenandrium arnoldii include relief from fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, and it is often prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture.
